title: The Father in the Green Coat
caption: The gate creaked, and they all turned. A man stood there in a worn green overcoat, cap twisted nervously between both hands, eyes scanning the garden as though bracing for something he deserved. He had seen the notices around the neighbourhood — the ones about missing seeds — and had put it together slowly, painfully, in the way parents do when their children have done something they didn't quite understand themselves. He introduced himself with a single word: Dev's dad. Chloe stepped forward before Rosie could speak. She asked the question gently, and the man's face crumpled with a kind of ashamed relief when he heard his son's name said aloud without anger. "He didn't tell me," he said quietly. "I didn't know. I'm so sorry." It wasn't an excuse. It was the truth, and it landed differently because of that.
story_notes: Dev's father arrives after seeing the notices. He is ashamed and apologetic — he had no idea what his son had done.
